title: Add more information about the VM to podman machine list
body:
Now also showing:
* CPUs
* Memory
* DiskSize

```
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED      LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  qemu        2 hours ago  Currently running  1           2.147GB     10.74GB
```

comments.body: no objections here, just need to add [NO TESTS NEEDED] to your commit

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *afbjorklund*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Apparently humans can't handle binary, not sure if it looks better or worse with "mebibytes" and "gibibytes"

```
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED      LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  qemu        7 hours ago  Currently running  1           2GiB        10GiB
```

They are totally intermixed within the code and the documentation, so there's no untangling in near-time...

I made a JSON version as well, there I will just use raw bytes instead since it is the "only way to make sure".

```json
[
  {
    "Name": "podman-machine-default",
    "Default": true,
    "Created": "2021-09-22T10:20:55.836921395+02:00",
    "Running": true,
    "LastUp": "2021-09-22T17:34:38.4010968+02:00",
    "VMType": "qemu",
    "CPUs": 1,
    "Memory": "2147483648",
    "DiskSize": "10737418240"
  }
]
```

PR for that coming separately. Let's go with SI units, for this one.

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Binary_prefix
